column10: Q9_WorkedWell
column11: Q10_Improvements

column5: Selecting a service|Choosing a date/time|Entering details
column10: The homepage made sense and the main call-to-action stood out, so I could start quickly. I liked the consistent colours and that buttons felt responsive on my phone.
column11: Once in the flow, the calendar felt fiddly and I kept losing my place. Shorten the forms, add a simple progress bar, and explain briefly why each personal detail is needed.

column5: Selecting a service|Entering details|Confirming the booking
column10: Once I found the booking area, the steps themselves were short and the confirmation screen summarised everything neatly.
column11: Finding the start was the hardest bit; the label "Services" wasn"t obvious. Put a clear "Book appointment" button at the top and show a one-screen privacy summary in plain English.

column5: Choosing a date/time|Entering details|Confirming the booking
column10: The "confirm" step reassured me with a clear reference number and a visual tick, which felt professional.
column11: Navigation back to change a time was confusing and reloaded the page. Keep my inputs when I go back, and make the calendar scroll less jumpy on small screens.

column5: Selecting a service|Choosing a date/time|Reviewing information
column10: The health tips matched my profile reasonably well and images were sharp, which kept me engaged.
column11: Some of the tip text was long and a bit technical. Add short summaries, use bullet points, and surface reading time so I can decide whether to open it.

column10: Starting a booking was straightforward and I appreciated the large tap targets on mobile.
column11: The data-handling explanation felt like legalese. Provide a concise "how we use your data" box with icons, and allow guest checkout with optional account creation later.

column5: Selecting a service|Entering details|Confirming the booking
column10: Buttons were easy to spot and the confirmation email arrived instantly, which gave me confidence that the booking was saved.
column11: The forms asked for several fields without context. Add helper text near each input, distinguish optional from required, and reduce the number of steps.

column10: Time choice was quick and the review page matched what I"d entered, which reduced anxiety before confirming.
column11: Oddly, getting back to the homepage was harder than it should be. Keep the top navigation fixed and add breadcrumbs so I always know where I am.

column10: Once I reached the right page, the layout was clean and uncluttered.
column11: The journey to get there was confusing with too many similarly named links. Simplify the menu labels and provide a "Start here" button on the homepage.

column5: Choosing a date/time|Entering details|Confirming the booking
column10: The confirmation reference number was handy when I screenshotted it for my calendar.
column11: The back button reset earlier choices, which was frustrating. Preserve state across steps and add clearer error messages that tell me exactly what to fix.

column5: Selecting a service|Reviewing information|Confirming the booking
column10: The last two steps felt quick, and the confirm button had good contrast.
column11: It took too long to figure out where to begin. A single "Book now" entry point above the fold would remove the guesswork.

column10: I liked the colour scheme; it looks trustworthy without shouting.
column11: Calendar performance was poor on 4G and privacy wording was dense. Preload next/previous months and summarise privacy in a short panel with a link to the full policy.

column10: The review page matched my input exactly and the "edit" links worked.
column11: Navigation buttons moved position between screens. Keep primary actions in a fixed place and add a step counter so progress feels tangible.

column10: Once in the flow, the date chooser responded quickly and the slot list was readable.
column11: The service list used terms I wouldn"t expect. Add simple descriptions under each service and include examples.

column10: The fonts are clear and the tap targets are big, which reduced accidental taps.
column11: The calendar sometimes jumped back a month. Keep the current position stable and allow typing a date as an alternative.

column10: The confirmation felt reassuring and the ICS download was useful.
column11: Finding the booking flow from the homepage wasn"t intuitive. Put a prominent "Start booking" button and reduce link clutter in the header.

column5: Selecting a service|Entering details|Confirming the booking
column10: The confirmation email contained the time and clinic clearly.
column11: Too many required fields without explanation. Show "why we ask" tooltips and allow address lookup instead of manual entry.

column10: The review step allowed quick edits without losing progress.
column11: The calendar"s touch targets were small and close together. Increase spacing and support a list view of times for accessibility.

column5: Selecting a service|Choosing a date/time|Confirming the booking
column10: The flow was short once started and the wording was plain.
column11: The initial menu labels were ambiguous. Replace jargon with everyday terms and show a short intro panel the first time I open the app.

column5: Choosing a date/time|Entering details|Confirming the booking
column10: I liked that the final screen recapped everything so I didn"t second-guess myself.
column11: The data-handling text felt like fine print. Offer a 4-bullet summary with icons and a link to more detail.

column10: The review page visually grouped information well.
column11: The services page was visually dense. Add more spacing, section headings, and a quick filter.

column5: Choosing a date/time|Entering details|Confirming the booking
column10: Some health tips felt spot on for me and used friendly examples.
column11: The booking journey itself felt heavy. Reduce the number of screens and let me save progress if I need to step away.

column10: Copy on the confirmation was in plain English and avoided medical jargon.
column11: Navigation hid when I scrolled, so I lost orientation. Keep a sticky header with a clear "Home" link.

column5: Choosing a date/time|Reviewing information|Confirming the booking
column10: The date chips were easy to tap and the confirm step was quick.
column11: The first screen didn"t tell me what would happen next. Add a short three-step overview at the start.

column10: Branding is calm and consistent, which helped a little.
column11: The route to booking was circuitous. Add a start button above the fold and simplify the mega-menu.

column10: I appreciated that the phone field accepted spaces and international formats.
column11: The calendar sometimes returned "no slots" without explanation. Show why, and suggest the nearest alternative clinic or time.

column10: The review table made it simple to double-check details.
column11: The service names were similar and I had to guess. Add short descriptions and an information icon beside each one.

column5: Choosing a date/time|Entering details|Confirming the booking
column10: I liked getting the reference number immediately.
column11: Data-handling language felt long and formal. Provide a concise summary with headings like "What we collect" and "How long we keep it".

column5: Selecting a service|Choosing a date/time|Confirming the booking
column10: The final page provided clear next steps, including how to reschedule.
column11: The homepage carousel pushed booking links down. Remove the carousel or move it below primary actions.

column5: Choosing a date/time|Entering details|Confirming the booking
column10: The visual confirmation felt solid and gave me a sense of completion.
column11: Going back to change one detail made me re-enter everything. Preserve inputs and provide inline validation hints.

column10: Some tips hit the mark and felt written for ordinary readers, not clinicians.
column11: Tips pages were long walls of text. Add headings, summaries, and an estimate of reading time.

column10: The date list displayed soonest slots at the top which sped things up.
column11: The entry page hid the booking call-to-action. Put it in the top section and reduce the number of competing links.

column10: The review screen kept the important actions in the same place across devices.
column11: The privacy statement needs a plain-language rewrite and a short summary card before the full policy.

column10: Once I reached the calendar, picking a slot was fast and obvious.
column11: I circled the homepage a few times first. Provide a single start button and shrink secondary content.

column10: The typography made long labels readable even on a small screen.
column11: The date picker was sensitive to minor swipes. Increase tap areas and include a manual date entry option.

column10: The confirmation gave me a calendar file which I appreciated.
column11: The earlier screens stacked too much information. Break content into shorter screens and use collapsible sections.

column5: Selecting a service|Entering details|Confirming the booking
column10: Colour contrast is good; headings are legible.
column11: The journey to start a booking felt obscure. Simplify the navigation and reduce the number of clicks to get going.

column10: The final summary table was tidy and shareable.
column11: Navigation disappeared while scrolling. Keep a sticky header and add a breadcrumb trail.

column5: Selecting a service|Choosing a date/time|Confirming the booking
column10: The steps were short and used familiar words once I started.
column11: The services list was long and ungrouped. Add categories and a search field.

column5: Choosing a date/time|Entering details|Confirming the booking
column10: I liked the confidence of the final confirmation screen.
column11: The privacy copy was heavy. Use icons and headings to summarise key points, with the full policy behind a link.

column10: The "edit" links worked perfectly without losing context.
column11: The label "service" could be clearer. Use everyday language and examples like "flu jab", "check-up" etc.

column5: Choosing a date/time|Entering details|Confirming the booking
column10: Some tips gave practical examples I could act on immediately.
column11: Switching months lagged and sometimes returned to the start. Preload nearby months and keep my place on return.

column10: The layout felt modern and consistent, which reduced guesswork.
column11: Form validation only showed after submit. Provide inline hints and accept common phone number formats.

column10: The end-to-end process was short once I found it, and the outputs were clear.
column11: The homepage contained too many competing items. Prioritise the booking journey and demote non-essential content.

column10: The high-contrast buttons helped once I was in the flow.
column11: The starting route was not obvious and required guesswork. Provide a guided "First time? Start here" card.

column10: The confirmation included next steps like "how to reschedule", which I liked.
column11: Field order felt random. Group related items and reduce the total number of required fields.

column5: Selecting a service|Choosing a date/time|Confirming the booking
column10: The slot list was concise and easy to compare.
column11: The service names felt technical. Add layperson descriptions and example scenarios to guide selection.

column5: Choosing a date/time|Entering details|Confirming the booking
column10: Emails landed instantly and were formatted clearly for mobile.
column11: The back button wiped entries. Keep state between screens and add a "save and finish later" option.

column10: I could review and correct details without losing my progress.
column11: Earlier screens crammed too much text. Use headings, bullets, and white space to make it scannable.

column10: Some tailored tips were useful and used plain English.
column11: The initial journey isn"t obvious. An onboarding overlay with three short pointers would help first-time users.

column5: Choosing a date/time|Entering details|Confirming the booking
column10: The confirmation step felt polished and gave me confidence.
column11: Privacy language felt intimidating. Offer a short, readable version with key points and a toggle to expand details.

column10: Once I reached the calendar, the rest was smooth and quick.
column11: The path to get there was confusing. Reduce menu depth and add a persistent "Book now" button.

column10: The form inputs handled copy/paste well, which sped things up.
column11: Explain why certain fields are required and collapse advanced options behind a disclosure control.

column10: The end was straightforward and I liked the clean summary.
column11: The privacy text read like a contract. Provide a human-readable overview with links to deeper content.

column5: Choosing a date/time|Entering details|Confirming the booking
column10: The visual design is calm and professional.
column11: Scrolling journey was long and I lost track. Add a visible step indicator and shorten each screen.

column5: Selecting a service|Choosing a date/time|Entering details
column10: Action buttons had good colour contrast which helped on a sunny day.
column11: The calendar sometimes returned empty results. Offer alternative locations or dates automatically.

column10: The final screen provided exactly what I needed to store the appointment.
column11: The privacy policy needs a short version. Add clear headings and bullet points at the top.

column10: I appreciated the accessible font size; nothing felt tiny.
column11: Finding the flow was the worst bit. Make "Book" the primary action and move news/promos lower down.

column10: The review/confirm steps were quick and I liked the ICS file download.
column11: Earlier, error messages were vague. Explain exactly what format a field needs and validate as I type.

column10: The slot picker was intuitive and the confirm step didn"t waste time.
column11: The services screen felt like a wall of text. Use categories and short descriptions.

column11: The middle of the journey was slow and repetitive. Reduce data entry and allow postcode lookup for address.

column10: I liked that field labels stayed visible while typing.
column11: The order of questions wasn"t logical. Group personal details and move optional items later.

column10: The last steps were efficient and predictable.
column11: The first step didn"t set expectations. Add a simple "1. Choose time 2. Enter details 3. Confirm" banner.

column11: Too many steps and the back button reset entries. Preserve state and allow "save for later".
